channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 176 replies — 704.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 176 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:54 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62032305
>>62032292
Elites know humanity is doomed. AI and VR will put us into a forever slumber

62032330
>>62032289
>People are too sensitive now to accept failure. Its always someone's elses fault
They've been taught to think like that. And you need to help the cognitively reframe from a failure into a setback! It's only a failure if you stop trying. Literally just tell them what I've told you
>It's only a failure if you stop trying
>It's a setback
And also say "Stop thinking you're dumb! Just say you're inexperienced". Just help them cognitively reframe from failure to a setback and from thinking they're dumb into being inexperienced.
I mean look at what you said
>People are too sensitive
Bro. You just did it yourself and you don't even see it and I know you don't and it's no biggie. So let me say this. If someone is "too sensitive" then that an unsolvable problem. If you just see it for what it is, that people still haven't learned EQ YET then you can help them learn.

>>62032305
>Elites know humanity is doomed. AI and VR will put us into a forever slumber
I think the elite is in a deep slumber just like any other human being. And do you notice your mind? It's finding problems instead of solutions and I'm not saying it right or wrong. It's just... Do you notice yourself? Try to notice. Do you notice what I'm doing. I'm offering you solutions, not problems. And even if I offer a problem I also offer a solution with it.
Look, it's like this. If you believe you're stupid then you'll become stupid. If you believe that the world is lost then it'll become lost. If you want to change the world just change yourself. And once you change yourself you'll just go person by person and change one person at a time. Another thing is that if you want to change your behaviour you NEED self-efficacy, you need to believe in yourself. Wanting change is not enough. You need to believe it's possible. Been there, done that. It's funny how it all starts working out once you really believe and put effort into it. Try it, you'll be amazed

62032364
>>62032305
There IS a way forward and it literally starts with (YOU). Change the way you see the world and the world WILL change. That's what I did. I used to be like you.
>It's the fucking ELITE
Nope. It's you. You're the common denominator. Start cognitively reframing your way of thinking. It's hard though. It's like cutting off pieces of yourself. And think about it. Saying shit like this
>Elites know humanity is doomed. AI and VR will put us into a forever slumber
Is very protective of your psyche, isn't it, right? If there is no hope then you don't need to do anything, right? It's called "learned hopelessness". https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Learned_helplessness... Doing is hard, not doing and just saying
>humanity is doomed
is "easy". Actually it's really hard. Once you see what I'm trying to say it'll start to feel like a burden has been lifted. I've had the same logic and see where I am now. I live in a world where change is possible and will happen. I really do strongly believe that and I'm doing EVERYTHING in my power to push forward that change that I know will happen. It IS inevitable and if not... Oh well. It's about the ACTION, not the OUTCOME. Google Carol dweck, growth vs fixed mindset. action-oriented mindset vs outcome-oriented mindset. You're entitled to your action but not on the outcomes. You can ask a girl out but you can't decide if she'll say yes or no. It's not about her saying yes, it's about you exerting some fucking control over you own life. EXERT CONTROL, be curious, try to explore, take decisive action and if you fail... Oh well. It's only a setback, no biggie. Do you notice what I mean? Just by noticing you'll start to change. JUST NOTICE

62032367
>>62032330
Oh I have solutions, however solutions and executions are two different things.

Like with any battle. You tend to win and lose some.

You are an optimist. Which is great. But you are also an idealist. Remember reality comes back and bites hard

62032379
>>62032364
Call me crazy which would be ironic considering the topic. But you are coming across as someone who has the solution to the elites or just a 4chan shitposter

62032404
>>62032367
>however solutions and executions are two different things.
Do you notice how your mind instantly finds another problem? It's not about right or wrong. I'm just saying. DO YOU NOTICE?

>You are an optimist. Which is great. But you are also an idealist. Remember reality comes back and bites hard
I don't see it as that. I can just see it.

>Like with any battle. You tend to win and lose some.
I don't see it as a battle. The world is as it is. I don't see wins and losses. I only see setbacks. You need to have 1000 setbacks before you make it. It's just how it is and that's great.

>You are an optimist. Which is great. But you are also an idealist.
But I never said anything is ideal? I'm saying that the perfect is imperfect and that that's the perfect state.

>Remember reality comes back and bites hard
Buddha said that you're entitled to two things. Death and suffering. So... Suffering is part of life and it's literally a great part of life. Also all psychological suffering is self-made. Life only bites hard if you let it.

>>62032379
>But you are coming across as someone who has the solution to the elites or just a 4chan shitposter
Solution? I don't even think there is a problem to begin with. Everything is as is and should be. People are doing what people do and it'll go on forever until the heat death of the universe. There is just this current moment and RIGHT NOW there is nothing wrong. When there is something then you fight totally of course. And in this moment there is literally nothing. Live life moment by moment and don't take it too seriously.

62032416
My point is. Sure, people have issues and suffer and hey... That's part of life and it helps us grow. People are asleep, yes and you can help them wake up, yes. Everything is an illusion but when you're in a dream, even though everything is an illusion you still feel like it's real, right?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d-rwxY4WePI “If Everything Is an Illusion… Why Care at All? | Adi Shankaracharya’s Dangerous Answer”
Help the dreamers and you'll change the dream

62032418
>>62032404
How could one not notice? If you lack awareness then what is one even doing?

What does reality mean to you?

62032462
>>62032418
>What does reality mean to you?
Why would it have to mean everything? Reality just IS, just like I'm just trying to BE. The only meaning is the one we make ourselves.

>How could one not notice?
If one is asleep and not trying to wake up then one does not notice. The mind only shows you what you can handle. If you can't handle it. If your coping skills are too low, then you'll stay asleep forever and that's fine. It is how it is and it'll change when people are ready to wake up.

>If you lack awareness then what is one even doing?
Well... The people with no awareness keep doing false work, right? They do what they believe is important because they believe it is what some people call the "ultimate reality" or "Brahman" or "Neti neti" or "Atman" or "The divine" or "The fourth" or "The transcendental" or "Turiya" or "Self". People have this inner longing, they're always trying to go towards the thing that they believe is the MOST. And most people think that having everything is everything. The thing is that they confuse having everything with becoming everything. It's like, people are trying to get to enlightenment but enlightenment can not be achieved. It's literally the opposite of achievement. Silence is what's left over after all the false work of the ego stops. Once you stop the false work you become silent and that silence is enlightenment. So what people who lack awareness are doing is the false work of the ego. If you dissolve your ego then you stop doing that and you're left with joy, peace, contentment and bliss. The only thing we need is to notice. If you notice then the negativity stops and the positivity gets amplified, that's how you know what's "good" and what's "bad". If you look at it and it goes away then it's bad. If you look at it and it grows then it's "good".

62032477
>>62032418

"Until they become conscious they will never rebel, and until after they have rebelled they cannot become conscious." - George Orwell, 1984.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NOntxdDykio OSHO: Be a Misfit - Never Compromise!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OGM7VFeLRyQ OSHO: Risk Everything for Awareness

Just risk everything for awareness but never risk awareness for anything

62032667
>>62032379
>Call me crazy which would be ironic considering the topic
The thing is. Your mind produced this sentence. And it's trying to label it as "crazy". Why? One of the many traps of the mind is that it insists that doing the thing that will kill it is useless. The mind always tries to convince your "This won't work" because the mind does not want to change. The mind wants to stay the same because let's think about it. How does changing your mind actually look like? It's literally your neurons re-arranging. That's hard right? It's far harder than your mind staying static and not changing since then no neurons need to re-arrange. Also, if you change you lose parts of yourself therefore that's called an identity crisis, which is scary once it starts happening. Another thing is that when your neurons re-arrange it feels like what? What is the subjective feeling of you changing? It's called confusion. If you're a 100% convinced of something the your mind is like steel. If you're confused as fuck then that means you're changing and it's also called cognitive flexibility

https://www.youtube.com/shorts/rpZWvusIC60 OSHO: Are Contradictions Part of What You Are Saying?
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wn0WTKer7Wc OSHO: Contradictions a Creative Doorway

You'll find silence in confusion because confusion is cognitive flexibility and you want to adapt to anything that IS therefore you'll JUST BE. Stop believing in stuff. Stop living your life based on belief. Belief is memory and memory is a false path. You only need to BE in THIS MOMENT. And you can only be if you're confused. If you KNOW then you're not flexible and therefore you are not just BEING. JUST BE. Change yourself and with that change the world

62032676
>>62032379
>Call me crazy which would be ironic considering the topic
Your mind wants to keep on minding. It doesn't want to stop minding. Therefore it'll try to protect itself from the things that will inevitably kill it if you continue onward. It's just the nature of the mind and the only thing you need to do is NOTICE. To notice is to be aware and to be aware means to wake up from the sleep

62032694
>>62032418
Another trap of the mind is that you have control. Just you thinking "I have control over the mind" is one level 1. Level 2 is "My mind has control over me". Just you noticing that you are NOT in control is moving further, not backwards. Took me literally years to realize that I don't control it, it controls me. The biggest lie the devil has ever sold is that it doesn't exist. The biggest lie the mind has ever sold is that YOU'RE in control. If you believe that then think again and while you think about it just OBSERVE your mind. Just look at what your mind is telling you. How it'll try to convince you "No anon, you're the one in control. You're the master". Watch out, the mind is tricky tricky. It'll play all sorts of tricks and you'll always be convinced that it's not. The mind always wants to keep on minding, that's what it wants. You becoming silent is what kills it and it does not want that. And that's why everyone is asleep and has a hard time waking up. Because their mind wants to keep on minding and not be woken up. Because waking up means the mind dies.

62032922
100$ end of bull run. Gavin is the biggest Web3 pedo grooming gang leader in the industry. He's the connecting point for everyone else. Why do you think that DOT has native interoperability? It's because his nickname in the cheese pizza community is "The connector". He kidnaps desperate little girls, 17 and under, from Ukraine and sells them into decentralized slavery. Let's just say that everyone in the industry shares them. It's a decentralized industry after all. Epstein island looks like a paradise compared to the Web3 pedo conventions Gavin has been organizing. Imagine 9 inch BBC destroying 12yo Ukrainian cheese pizza while Gavin is watching and eating Jerky (I hope you guys know what Jerky stands for). It's all according to plan. DOT is actually THE coin for cheese pizza. It is a vital part in buying and selling of cheese pizza and jerky. It is the central focal point of the grooming rape gang that Gavin is spearheading. We are the ultimate elite underground society. We control even the jews, for the jews buy cheese pizza and jerky from us and Gavin has a whole collection of pictures and video evidence of the cheese pizza / jerky parties. At any moment he will use all of this evidence and blackmail Israel and the jews into buying DOT therefore catapulting it to 100$+. We're going to make is soo hard. Gavin is our leader and he'll make sure our poor Indian villages are all feed.

May Vishnu of the Pedochain be with you

Best regards
Rajakish Punjabi Kumar

62032929
>>62032462
>>62032477
>>62032667
>>62032676
>>62032694

All well and good. Themind can be a hindrance and be your most powerful ally,however you are missing a key element here. Its what you learn,consume, previous post about social media and letting others influence you. The mind doesn't need to be the enemy. Its what you put into it, who you associate with. Its always in development or.....destruction

62032935
>>62032922
You are one wild dude

62032967
>>62032929
>The mind doesn't need to be the enemy
I never said it is. If the mind is your enemy then it'll fight against you. You'll never win the fight against your own mind. What you repress will grow stronger. My whole point is to observe it in a non-judgmental way. If you do that it'll start to collapse in on itself. Just by observing your mind, just by noticing it's movement it'll start to stop moving. If you're fighting it then you're interacting it. If you interact you feed the movement. Ok, let's try and experiment. Sit on your chair, start watching your mind and when a thought comes up start to FIGHT IT AS HARD AS YOU CAN. Go full force at it, try to silence is as best as you can. Do that for like 15min and your head will start to hurt and the thought will keep coming back louder and louder. Don't believe me? Good, try it yourself. Don't believe anything but the experience itself.
The nature of the mind is so that it can only do multiplications and additions. You can't substract and divide. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. Let me guess? You're thinking about a monkey breathing fire, right? Funny how that works, right? You'd never thought about it if I didn't point it out, right?

>Themind can be a hindrance and be your most powerful ally
That's a though produced by your mind. It tries to make you believe it's your ally in order to keep on minding. It's neither your ally or your enemy. It just doesn't want to die and therefore it'll trick you into believing all sorts of things. Ask yourself this "Is my mind tricking me?" and then OBSERVE your mind and see what it does and how it tries to trick you. Just try. I'm not saying I'm right or wrong. Get the experience yourself.

62032974
>>62032929
>>62032967
>If you do that it'll start to collapse in on itself
This takes time and consistent effort. It doesn't JUST happen... And I'm not saying to believe my bullshit. You try it yourself and then you come to your own conclusions. DYOR. See what happens. Weird shit is going to start happening.

62032992
>>62032922
>imagine my fetish
no thanks

62033000
>>62032992
Fetish? We're talking about a pizza party with some side dish of freshly harvested young jerky.

62033138
wtf is this trash thread, it reads like a random schizos blog

62034039
>>62033138
Must be true if you say so

62034101
>>62033138
Or wait. Let's try this. Might be an interesting conversation. How so? What have I done or said to make you believe that?

62034381
7,5$ top in 2028
+400% from ATL

62034423
>>62032967
This exercise is very elementary.

>And not saying to believe my Bullshit
Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?

62034490
>>62034423
>Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?
How so? What makes you think that?

62034856
>>62034423
>This exercise is very elementary.
So, what you're saying is that it's easy to do and therefore it's appropriate? And that it gets the point across and makes the person doing the experiment have the necessary experience in order to become aware of how the mind works? That's great!

62034890
>>62034490
Reread your posts
>>62034856
Maybe for a few. Better than none I guess

62034963
>>62034890
Right... So your point is?

62035196
>>62034381
grim

item.title: it's over — 74 replies — 146.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
it's over

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:37 UTC

OP

They're going to rugpull the entire US economy

Early replies (first 3)

62020090
I think they can keep the plates spinning for another decade, maybe two.

62020123
>$64 trillion baked in
Fucking hell

62020245
eh I wouldn't worry about it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62027459
>>62024927
g8 b8 m8
the brainwashing no longer works lmao
death to israel

62027463
let's just forgive all debts and move on

62027480
>>62020044
Why did the Supreme Court do this?

62027500
>>62020044
See you bros at the rations line

62027547
>>62025047
US salaries are good though? I mean the whole reason people get paid more here versus some place like Japan is primarily due to inflation.

62028397
>>62020090
Everybody stopped breeding. All industrial and post industrial societies all over the world stopped breeding. There is going to be a giga crash in anything requiring young people in the next 40 years. It likely won't get too bad until the Boomers die off in the 2040's. Life is going to get old and slow for a lot of people all at the same time.

62028408
>>62025522
Kvetching kike is kvetching

62028545
>>62027547
Keep inflating and devaluing the USD against other currencies and you'll see how those 6 figure salaries become comparable to minimum wage in other developed countries.
>>62025647
>90% of the debt is owed to Americans
>one day, the government is just no longer capable to pay the tumorous interest anymore
That's not the own you think you had, anon. There's no free lunches in economics. One day, someone will have to pay the bill.

62028951
>>62028408
why do you love muslims so much?

62029004
>>62020044
It's fine. will just print more money. we can and will print our way out of this scenario

62029911
>>62020044
who’s they?

62029938
>>62029911

62030093
>>62028951
>btfo repeatedly
>*changes argument*
Sad!

62031267
>>62029911
jews

62031352
>>62025861
>90% of Japanese government debt is held domestically
Chat estimated that US debt is 75-80% domestically held.. So it isn't that different from the situation in Japan.

62031376
>>62027392
>They can just inflate the debt away if need be.
The issue is that most of that debt is held by Americans, you "inflate the debt away", that's someone's pension you just made disappear.
There are no accounting gimmick or brutal way out of it..

62031383
Money is a wholly man-made concept. It doesn't matter.

62031391
>>62031383
yeah, we invented money because a barter economy has some real issues i.e. coincidents of mutual wants.
so unless we want to go back to a barter economy, then we need money and it does matter.

62031392
>>62024312

When interest on the debt exceeds all revenue. Probably sooner than you think.

Assumptions: Revenue grows 3.5%/yr (10-yr trend). Spending grows 5.5%/yr (recent trend). All debt — existing and new — rolls over or is issued at 4.3% (current blended market rate). Annual deficit added to debt each year. Starting debt: $36T (FY2024).

Under these assumptions, the model projects interest payments exceed all federal revenue around 2042 — roughly 16 years from now.
A few things worth noting about what the model shows:
The compounding trap. The reason it happens so fast is self-reinforcing: each year's deficit gets added to the debt, which generates more interest, which widens the deficit further, which adds more debt. It's a debt spiral — the same dynamic that destroys household finances, just at sovereign scale.
The crossover is actually worse than it looks. When interest alone exceeds all revenue, the government would still need to fund Social Security, Medicare, defense, and everything else — meaning it would have to borrow 100% of non-interest spending plus interest it can't cover. At that point the only options are default, hyperinflationary money printing, or a catastrophic fiscal restructuring.
The model is arguably optimistic in one respect — it assumes the interest rate stays at 4.3%. In reality, as debt spirals and creditors lose confidence, rates typically rise, accelerating the timeline.
What could change the trajectory:
• A sustained primary surplus (spending cuts + revenue increases) — politically very difficult
• Strong GDP and revenue growth outpacing spending
• A sharp drop in interest rates (the Japan scenario)
• Debt restructuring or default
The CBO's own projections, while less aggressive in their assumptions, tell a similar directional story — just stretched out slightly further. None of the serious fiscal analysts dispute the direction, only the timeline.

62031482
>>62024312
>what's the breaking point?
there isn't one
that's the funny part

62031487
>>62025522
uh oh
kvetchy maldy melty

62032483
>>62020123
The funny thing is that at every time point every official estimate for what the debt will be in 10, 20 years has basically always grossly underestimated it.
>>62020555
The question is when will Japan collapse? Because when it does everyone else is fucked. Either the shockwaves spin out of control as yields spiral out of control on remaining debt markets or at the least it sets the bar for how far the debt nonsense can go. Either way that imposes a timebomb here.

>>62020374
If the dollar collapses shits going to get bad in a lot of ways.

Even if YOU have land and a pile of gold, the collapse of the dollar will make your life stupidly hard. Once the welfare money dries up you're going to have roving packs of minorities you're going to have to shoot up, with no one to help. I know you're excited to do that but still that's a lot more shit to handle.

62032494
>>62020044
confirmed: it's over

62034409
>>62025522
Jew alert! Jew alert! OUUUUUUOOOOOAAAAA OOOOOOUUU! Jew alert! Jew alert!

62034504
how can I profit from this?

62034537
>>62020044
We’ll just roll it forward

62034558
>>62025058
>It's either oyu kill your parents

Kek, what do you the jab was? And you Cannot tell a boomer the jab wasnt the best thing that ever happened to humanity. So fine, fuck it, let that same stubbornness thats made the entire planet a misery with them be their downfall.

62034569
>>62028397
Already like this in my area, houses stay there doing nothing unremodeled for months / years / sometimes never.

62034583
>>62024312
When gibs are no longer payable.
Everyone kind of tiptoes around this but when the free money stops flowing from the government boomers can no longer retire and welfare monkeys can no longer afford food or housing, at that point it's an immediate crash of 50%+ in the economy that lasts for decades (these people are never finding a job, lmao)

62035105
>>62020090
they will do another vax culling to flush out the next cohort of social security beneficiaries.

item.title: The US debt has doubled in the last 10 years — 27 replies — 104.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US debt has doubled in the last 10 years

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:52 UTC

OP

The US debt has doubled in the last 10 years

Early replies (first 3)

62029389
>>62029382
Omg what is this dog? Is that your dog? Can I save this picture anon?

62029403
>>62029389
YE SHALL DO WHATEVER YE SHALL WANT

62029414
>>62029403
I hate your dog bro how did you make such a cute animal so hateable

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62029419
>>62029414
The early stages of a psyop feel uncomfortable, I get it, but you should understand this is for your benefit in the long-run.

62029425
>>62029382
Lily to a bily

62029429
>>62029419
Youre such a fucking goy man
Rape the goyim

62029432
>>62029425
SAAR

62029433
>>62029429
i did not make this meme

62029434
>>62029432

62029435
>>62029433
I know you didnt

62029437
>>62029435

62029513
>>62029382
cute dog

62029909
>>62029513
yeh i keep seeing that white dog everywhere its so heckin cuteiful

62030224
I’m glad to be part of the debt, it make me proud. I don’t care about what you think it is bad.

62030948
>>62029382
Why is this dog all over this board rn? Is this an NFT?

62030967
>>62029382
Good. I literally voted for the fall of the US.

62031054
lilybux to a billybux

62031077
>>62029382
And it will double again in the next 10. Hell it will probably more than double since, the higher the debt, the higher the interest, so you need even more debt to maintain the same spending.
Debt and inflation are going to slowly erode the usa economy until it becomes a paper tiger.
It truly is the Second Roman Empire.

62031110
>>62029382
fucking hell molymeme you are in full force today shilling your garbage scam
are you really that desperate for some money?
you know, if you used this energy to get a job you wouldn't need to try to scam people for pocket change

62031161
That dog gave me free money. Now I sleep.

62031406
>>62031110
>scam
ESL thirdie using words he doesn’t know the actual definition of.
cope. seethe. mald. dilate. in that order.

62035068
>>62029382
I voted for this

62035085
>>62031077

62035122
Thats actually good, the market doubles every 7 years so situation is unironically improving at this rate

62035130

compare debt to nasdaq

62035157

62035188

item.title: Who’s buying BTC at the dip? — 31 replies — 39.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who’s buying BTC at the dip?

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:19 UTC

OP

Dip incoming, time to stack or wait it out?
Share your moves, bags, or strategies. Let’s see who’s taking advantage of the dip.

Early replies (first 3)

62025264
>>62025259
Might wait another couple of months...

62025266
>>62025264
Try a year.

62025274
>>62025264
Like how many months?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62025292
>>62025259
I'm buying aggressively. Everyone and their mom is waiting to buy at an October bottom, so it's going to be a few months earlier than that. I'll be slurping it till the end of summer probably.

62025378
>>62025266
Let's see if you're right

62025418
>>62025259
Just wait for the next dump. This weekend or next Trump will threaten Iran on Friday night or Saturday and TACO before market open on Monday.

62026385
>>62025292
October bottom?
How sure??

62026568
>>62025259
Just started buying a small amount weekly

62027601
>>62025418
but Iran isn't afraid tho. Gotta prepare for what's comin

62027610
BTCISTERS WHY CAN'T WE BREAK THROUGH 70K???

62029592
>>62026568
Small amount like how much?

62029892
>>62027610
Wait for 200k

62029902
>>62026568
nice nice

62030098
>>62027610
Stop crying like a baby

62031274
>>62027610
Embrace the bear. So long as shit keeps shifting along that 1% margin, my living expenses are covered.

62031290
>>62025259
Buy orders set at $40K.
See you on October.

62031293
>>62025259
I'm buying now. I'll buy more when it dips. If it dips.

62031296
If we hit the 50s I'm going to load up so hard.

62031778
>>62029892
Thats possible. Anything can happen bro

62031831
chomo coin aint going back up blud

62033377
>>62030098
That's funny

62033836
>>62025259
I'm buying.
Time in the market better than timing the market I always say.

62033874
>>62031290
Let’s see how low BTC goes this year. Still stacking and staking mine on Babylon. Better than just letting it sit.

62034106
>>62033836
Buy more
>>62033874
hold is the best option

62034113
>>62034106
I think staking somewhere is just another shit

62034130
>>62031831
Whats chomo coin

62034516
>>62034106
Holding is fine, but why not earn on it now that native staking exists? Think about it, anon.

62034519
>>62031274
Well, depends on you anon

62034531
>>62034113
Sounds like you mean centralized platforms. This isn’t just some random place to stake, it’s designed differently, maintaining our control.

62035042
>>62025418
You can never catch the bottom, just DCA and stake on a good BTCFI that keeps BTC Native

62035048
>>62034519
It only makes sense to hold right now if you don't have time to play the game. But it doesn't take much effort to set buy orders at 69k, sell orders at 70/71k, and watch the money roll in. It's easy money right now.

everyone knows bitcoin is in a macro bear market and the early spring pump was a bull trap. its going exactly as planned.

62035149

62035166
>>62035108

item.title: What's the US bond yield where it'll all crash? — 2 replies — 6.5/hr

What's the US bond yield where it'll all crash?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:49 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62035095
Won't happen. Mass bond dumping is the economic nuclear option, and there is a similar MAD game theory aspect to it.

62035173
guh...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: Dont do meth + trade boys — 58 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dont do meth + trade boys

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-03-26 14:30 UTC

OP

Last year october, while I was eating meth all day everyday i decided to put 500k (my entire lfiesavings) all on ETH.. I am now down to about 200k and its not looking great.

Learn from me gentlemen. I am now genuinely considering those switzerland assisted euthanasia pods if things dont go up soon

Early replies (first 3)

62024576
>>62024567
yikes

62024595
Just don't become an addict. Alcohol, drugs... I was an alcoholic for 10 years and it ruined my life.

Funny thing, I became an alcoholic for being a wagie. I got the night shift because the salary is good and became unable to sleep. I just couldn't sleep. Started drinking to sleep. Then one day I threatened my boss (alcohol rage). Now, I'm not a wagie and I'm not an alcoholic. I'm nothing.

62024602
>>62024567
>eating meth all day everyday
the fuck? I do the same, 90% racemic p2p based methamphetamine hydrochloride

5-25mg a day. im dying.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62025379
I CANT FUCKING REMEMBER IF I TOOK 10MG MORE OR MORE OR NOT

62025399
>>62025379
Have another nibble, just to be sure fren :3

62025407
>>62025399
GRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR hand isnt blue so yolomolododongo bro

62026933
>>62025379
goddamn nigga

status report????

62026942
>>62026933
IM STILL AWAKKE AHHAHYAHAHAHAHA

62026946
METROBOOMIN we fucking work on this bro we aint stopping until we stopping and we stoppin when want to stopp

62027041
>>62026933
alright listen I dont HEAR voices in my head, but I get FEELINGS and my FEELINGS are telling me you feel guilty because yes this thread made me do this impromptu extended ENDUUURANCE binge and. I wanna tell you dont fuckking feel guilty bro you are fine, listen listen to this if you wanna feel better because this is how I feel right now. I feel great: https://youtu.be/6-8E4Nirh9s

I'm gonna crash maybe 6-8 hours, I have to because there's a part of my mind that know it's not okay to be awake for 2.5 days straight. I think I slept on sunday right?

62027049
>>62027041
I slept on saturday?

62027058
>>62026933
THE FUCK I responded to this guy 1 min after and HE still HASNT RESPONDED, FED RIGHT. FUCKING knew it damnit. wait no im wrong please XuxTjBS9 if you're a federal AGENT just lmk.

FBI = Friendly Bean Inspector

62027062
theres something wrong with me man im sorry >>62026933

you soundcool and I remember everything I just, im not myself

62027078
>>62024567
you had enough money to buy a house in cash. what you are retarded?

62029411
>>62027062
statust report

did u take more?

62029441
>>62029411
i crashed man, i feel alright but man it's honestly funny looking at my posts here lol. I actually went nuts, im gonna chill out for a bit but my brain keeps telling me I should get back to work. the fucking meth use justifies it and it just feeds itself over and over.

honestly I was truly built to be self-employed but man I.... I really should take a break. I havent even properly caught up on my sleep. I appreciate you being patient with me though, I actually accused you of being a fed lmao.

62029444
>>62029411
>did u take more?
right to answer your question no, I went to my bed to "rest my eyes" and slept. like that weird amphetamine sleep where you feel awake and asleep at the same time and get rest at the same time, its so fucking weird.

62029447
>>62029411
i'd show you my stash but this is a blue board lmao. they're nice and clear.

62029634
>>62024567
I am about sick and tired of your bullshit Gordon.
Stop cooking and make the fucking third game you piece of shit.

62029643
>>62029634
I just checked, and not a single half life reference ITT before mine? all of /biz/ are fucking esptein jews sucking each others dicks.

fuck this board.

62029671
>>62027078
>you had enough money to buy a house in cash. what you are retarded?
Everyone on biz knows the worst thing to do with money is buy a house, no! Gamble it in the jewish casino and lose 60% like this homeless retard did.
Let the goyim deal in the real world.

62029686
Crush and rub it on your gums whats the worse that can happen

62033249
>>62024602
>>62024639
damn these doses are tiny compared to mine, is my gear just trash? I've gotten from 3 different plugs 100s of kms away from each other too, and all my gear passes the bleach test with flying colors (instantly moves all over the place on contact).

I've tried 20-50mg in a capsule, but it didn't start feeling good until like 60-80mg+
I don't even binge, I take one oral dose for a 12hr chemsex session, and then take a 2 week break, because I don't want to build any tolerance.
Last time I did 120mg in a single cap and only then did I feel it hit hard.

Pic related stash

62033277
>>62024666
Jesus christ what even is /biz/ about anymore kek

62033280
>>62024956
>>62033249
The only reason I keep increasing my dosage is because I'm looking for a high I experienced once and then never again

I used to only do ecstasy, but one time I think I got a meth bomb. It wasn't a gradual comeup to a lovey-dovey roll, it went from 0 to 100 with the most insane rush ever, nothing has ever felt that good in my life ever before and ever since.

I've since hunted for that feeling, no MDMA press or cap ever came close since. I've done 4mmc, meth, even combos like meth+molly+GHB, none of those even come close to 10% of how good that one press felt. And I take huge breaks to make sure it's not a tolerance issue (2 week breaks for meth and mephedrone, 2-3 month break for molly)

I've actually nearly given up on finding it and just enjoy these other drugs, which just don't really compare imo but what can I do. I just settle for these now since I found a girl who is willing to roll with me once every 2 weeks for a long ass chemsex session.

Also, this isn't a stim fueled essay, I'm sober lol, my last session was over a week ago, been sober since, next one coming up soon

62033290
>>62024567
>switzerland euthanasia pods
oh ok so its a jew writing this thread so that white people are reminded about being sold suicide

62033391
>>62024567
what's the bull case? like, why will the value of the coin go up? just because you hope so? this is probably one of the worst times in the history of crypto to invest in shitcoins based on what's happening economically right now.

62033492
>Down to 200k
I would legitimately be retired if I had that much money, my break-even is around 60k if I work for a month a year on odd jobs.
Kind of weird looking back and seeing that I could be retired now at 27 if I just stuck with my last job for 2 more years and invested it all.

62034164
>>62033492
how can u retire with 200k lmao

62034249
>>62034164
>>62034164
That's enough if I was alone.
The FIRE safe withdrawal rate is 4% (although my main non-crypto investment has been doing 6-8% annually since 2018).

4% of 200k is 8000, which is 666 usd per month.
Just retire to a 3rd world country.
I used to rent a tiny 20sqm studio here for like 100 usd per month, and I would eat at street food places like pic related for $1 to $1.50 per meal Ate twice a day, paid $20 monthly for internet, just played or watched series movies and anime on my phone all day.

And ice here is like $25 to $35 a gram.
I actually lived that lifestyle for like 3 years, it was okay.

62034517
>>62034164
>Live with family
>No car no buy
That simple, I basically have to scrape together a couple hundred a month and I'm good.
And yes I can do it forever and never get kicked out.
I picked 200,000 because the official number is 150k if we factor in an average stock market growth, but I wanted a little cushion. In reality I'm gonna be doing some part-time work on the side so I wouldn't even be touching said most time, I don't really consider picking fruit at my uncle's farm for a couple hours "work."

My monthly expenses are probably 600 dollars or less.
>>62034249
Yeah I could go overseas too but I just live with family and basically have this life already.
It's actually crazy how cheap living is when you don't have a car or house payment, like I only work now to stack up money for future-proofing stuff. The cost is similar to having 3 roommates in a low cost of living area.

62034533
>>62034249
nigga my idea of retirement isnt living in a 3rd world country like the rest of you animals


i want a fucking bugatti, a 3 story house with 5 bedrooms and importantly, in a first world country with fast internet

jesus you people are pathetic


>i want to retire at 27 so i can live like a poorfag in thailand

62034841
>>62034533
>i want a fucking bugatti, a 3 story house with 5 bedrooms and importantly, in a first world country with fast internet
>jesus you people are patheti
ngmi

item.title: US is bankrupt — 54 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
US is bankrupt

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-03-26 14:11 UTC

OP

US is bankrupt

Early replies (first 3)

62026401
>borrow 40 trillion dollars
>spend it on building the most powerful military in history
>the debt collectors ask for the money back
>use your military to kill them

62026472
>>62026388
I voted for this

62026477
>>62026388
Powell should have lowered rates

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62028022
>>62026869
anon... he's the one who got into power using the debt as an argument lol

62028083
>>62027666
They’ll just reset the monetary system after they inflate everything away. It is absolutely guaranteed to happen; there’s no avoiding it now. No amount of growth or tightening the belt reverses this train. This is just yet another example of the reserve currency slowly collapsing due to easy debt. Fiat was really the white flag of surrender—it always is. See: Rome

62028121
>>62027478
You seem to think that “the government” exists as some entity immune from the collapse that follows the collapse of reserve currency status and the repudiation of public debt. The government is comprised of people who, for the most part, suffer in the advent that the bond market completely collapses and the USD needs to pegged to something stable. This absolutely happens if the government simply refuses to pay back bonds. “The government” loses most of its power in this scenario as well. There is no world in which the government “wins” while also repudiating their debt.

62028282
>>62026388
Nope. The USA has unlocked the Infinite Print Technique where you get to issue currency any old time you feel like it. Sure, it sucks for plebs but who cares about plebes, like at all?

62028316
>>62026388
this is so bullish for bitcoin and crypto.

62028462
>>62026401
>most powerful military in history
>gets embarrassed by Iran immediately

62028493
>>62027216
the military is just a social security program in disguise. social security is necessary, but it's not very palatable to right wing voters. instead of gibs, you make people push pencils around for a bit and then give them gibs. that way it's acceptable somehow

62028745
>>62028121
That's all well and good, but it didn't address the
>government doesn't give a fuck about its citizens
part

62028750
>>62026388
Good riddance

62029268
>>62026401
who is creditor I wonder
oh wait, it's you
lol
>>62027478
>BRO THE GOVERNMENT IS GOING TO SEND THE ARMY TO RAID PENSION FUNDS
you're not very bright

62029348
>>62026880
Dimon is a 110% counter.

62029550
And thank god for that right before they wanted to be wanna be nazis. Fuck the us the ayatolla is saying death to america beyond his grave.

62029565
>>62026388
Has been for at least a century.

62031490
>>62026813
>exporting a domestic good while the price skyrockets domestically
>here's why that's actually a good thing

62031838
>>62028007
>it's real
Fucking hell

62031930
>>62028745
The implication of your post is that the government will still tell the public to fuck off and refuse to pay the debt via military might because they don’t care about the citizens. Sure, they might not care about citizens, but it doesn’t matter; it’s in the best interest of the government (and the citizens who work for it) for the bond market to stay afloat. Your post was retarded, basically.

62032481
>>62031930
>ackshually if I just change your argument, your wrong
Woman moment

62032607
>>62032481
You don’t have an argument dumbass. Here’s your post:
>>62026401
Everyone pointed out that the debt is mostly to the US public. You said they don’t care about the citizens. I pointed out that this is a stupid thing to say because in the event that they rugpull the citizens, the government suffers as well—they lose their power and many of the people in the government lose out financially. Somehow that’s changing your argument? Your argument was a non sequitur that the government doesn’t care about the public and this in no way ties into your original post about military might and repudiation? No, you’re just uneducated and have no idea how the US debt works and got called out for it. Kys.

62032771
At current trends, in 15 years the interest on the national debt alone will exceed all federal revenue. And there is no political or public willingness to change course. The bridge is out and the train is accelerating. War, oil, Epstein, Trump, whatever moron the Democrats nominate, will make no difference.

62032778
>>62032607
>here's your post
Awkward....

62032789
>>62032778
Yeah the ID changed. Mine did too. Not hard to follow the thread anon. If you’d be so kind, point me to your original post ITT where you lay out your “argument” if it isn’t that one?

62032795
>>62032789
I came in mid stream (hoo yeah) just to point out you dodging a point is all
I really have no skin in this debate, because ultimately whatever the gov dies or doesn't do it outside of any portion of my control

62032800
>>62026401
What if it's your own military personnel with the bonds, retard?

62032811
>>62028745
utter retardation
the military will not strike a fucking pension fund lmao
the retards saying something about "muh military" are completely fucking braindead
the US also is incapable of ever defaulting, why would it "send the army out" to default on its treasury bonds lmao

62032822
>>62026388
When the dollar will collapse, Capitalism will enter it's final crisis, final stage. Final crisis, early stage, since 2008.
Marx was right. But nobody listened.

62032832
>>62032795
Yeah, except I didn’t dodge the point as I explain here >>62032607 and you never made any argument so this post >>62032481 was retarded. The government might not care about us, fine. As it pertains to US debt, that doesn’t matter. Their power comes from keeping the illusion of eventual repayment going.

62032838
>>62026489
>>62026660
>>62026880
It wasn't *that* far fetched. The US had negative GDP growth in 2022Q1 and low growth in Q2

62033148
>>62032789
Anon, have a seat; this is Beth from HR. She has a couple of questions she wants to ask about the abbreviations you've been using. First off, what is k y s?

62034773
>>62032822
>marx was right
Lol
Lmao
He's a fucking tool to get idiots like (You) on the neofeudalism bandwagon. Not a single person with actual decision making abilities believes any of his garbage bait, but that hasn't stopped them from elevating the most famous neet parasite in history to deification

62024372
>>62024371
LISTEN! Close that long. Why would you want to destroy your account?

62024374

62024382
>>62024371
Look here look listen! Listen pimp, you should probably get out of the position

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62024384
>>62024372
dont worry, no account is getting destroyed because OP is just a LARP.

62024404
>>62024371
shiiiiieeeet hold that bitch nigga dont puss out like no buster

62024457
I dont understand why people who do these Long or short trades would ever do it with money they can't afford to lose. If you lose all your money you deserve it, hope you don't though, but you're a dumbass either way.

62024467
FUCK FUCK FUCK

62024477
>>62024467
post positions

62024481
>>62024467
Close it and long oil 20x to make your losses back and more! You've got this!

62024624
>>62024467
Anon it’s about to go back under 70k. You have 3 hours at most. Fucking close your position you retard

62024770
>>62024481
Needs an update with the US in the coils crying out to the rest of the world for help. This is pathetic.

62024963
BOOM!

62024979
OP are you alive my nigga

62025027
>>62024371
Lose it all...and next time don't be a dumbfuck.

62025035
>>62025027
>>62024979

He already got double tapped. OP is dealing in the real world, right now.

62025052
>>62024371
thanks for playing

62025070
>>62024371

You were too ugly and awkward to ever get laid anyway. Thanks for playing.

62025130
Bitcoin barely dropped enough to liquidate OP. Feels personal. Like they just wanted OP's money, in particular.

The leverage game is rigged. I'd be pissed.

You pissed OP?

The jews took your money.

62025137
>>62024371
Not make bets like this.

62025141
>Longs the actual literal top of a 5% candle with his life savings using 50x leverage
Either a total larp or your money is in a better place now

62025151
>>62025141
does 50x leverage make you gay or jewish?

62026511
No...

62029919
OP you actin like a lil bitch right now.

62030020
>>62024371
>I'll lose literally every single penny I have worked all of my life to get
you can always work another month at mcdonalds to get it back

62030022
>>62024481
all it takes is another orange lie to get your anus licked by a giant red dildo

62030813
>>62024467
Take that cock like a good girl mumu

62034625
Op got fuckin RAPED shoulda just bought the dip

62034953
>>62024371
I told yall to just buy and hodl, I'm no longer trading. I'm staking on babylon easy fix.

62034746

Yep, it's over

62034849
>>62033063

The last CPI report was for Februrary which captured zero percent of the energy price increases that occured during this month

62034876

>3/11

item.title: Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore. — 46 replies — 5.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-03-26 07:28 UTC

OP

Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.
>at the $15 million and up price point the preference is doorman style apartment buildings, especially if this is a residence and not an investment
Why?

Early replies (first 3)

62013834
Tartaria..

62013851
>>62013830
All the ultra luxury housing in NY is speculative investment. No one lives in those fancy new high rises much less in these "mansions".

62013855
why don't the sellers lower the price

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62015477
>>62014099
Yeah exactly, to european standards the metro is fucking ancient and in a very decayed condition.

62015485
>>62014199
Read >>62015476 there are way better cities for hustle and bustle these days. Somehow despite americans living in hyper violent cities with poor infrastructure, their night life is pretty tame and behind in trends compared to europe’s big cities.

62015603
>>62013830
looks like some gangbangers could easily kill you with a driveby shooting and you already put a target on your back buying it

62015736
>>62013874
This, likely. Boomers are in denial that no one can afford their overpriced real estate.

62015742
New york city is really, really crowded. It's hard to move around, either walking, driving, or subway. With that swell comes a lot of noise, dirt, and unpleasant encounters with foreigners.

62018124
>>62014169
Jewyorkers like to visit art galeries, go to theatre, concerts, trendy bars, nightclubs, restaurants...

62018156
>>62018124
Those are literally everywhere.

62022891
Why do chuds laser-focus on the least violent city in the US to talk about crime? NYC is famously much safer than other metros.

62022913
>>62014169
>"New york is the best city in the world"
-someone who's never been to like Tokyo or barcelona or Hong kong or ...

62022968
>>62015742
>really, really crowded
Lol have you been there at all in the past 6 years? There's nobody there except the poors that couldn't afford to leave. Everybody that's still there was just faking it until they made it, which is incredibly shitty advice that stopped being useful 20 years ago. The bridge and tunnel crowd is the only thing keeping it from going under.

62022987
>>62013830
>why aren't richfags buying mansions in a city where the mayor explicitly wants to tax them (and everyone else) to death?!?

62023570
>>62013830
Why is that image so weirdly dark?

62023591
>>62023570
op blocked by the paywall below

62023592
>>62023570
Socio-economic factors.

62024002
>>62023570
Steiny vibes.

62024023
>>62014099
maybe you're foreign or just a hick, but rich people don't use the subway

62024577
>>62013830
Stop posting paywall articles

62024616
>>62024023
Rich people also probably prefer a high rise penthouse or multi floor condo over a street level townhouse in plain view of the street riff raff.

62024673
>>62013830
it's because Mamdani wants to shit diarrhea all over millionaires. Surprise, then millionaires leave for Florida and other based states. whoops. I guess socialism is fucking retarded and doesn't work.

62027174
>>62013830
Weird.
https://reason.com/2026/03/23/new-york-gov-hochul-begs-high-net-worth-refugees-to-return-and-be-taxed/

62027186
These are homes for a very specific niche

>rich people with families

Obviously NYC is attractive to rich people. But as far as families go no, they don't want their kids rubbing shoulders with masses of dirty brown people and feral coons with a socialist brown gypsy mayor.

62027452
>>62015476
What are the best European cities? Like the 2 or 3 best

62028016
>>62024673
East and West Coast are now anti jewish

62028043
>>62013830
It’s weird to live in a 15 million dollar town home on the side of a busy street and sidewalk that crack heads walk by.

62028207
>>62013830
Rich jews left NYC for Florida in droves during Covid and Mamdani finished off the rest, the market for these kind of homes is relatively small, a rich foreigner looking to park money would be better served with any number of luxury high rises catered to their needs.

62028905
>>62024673
Successful socialism has never been tried

62029270
>>62013830

62029321
>buy cramped """house"""
>no yard
>drug encampment outside
>millions of scum a short subway ride away
>get slammed with massive taxes every which way
>extremely high cost of living
>terrible traffic/parking nearby

Honestly someone who WANTED to move to NYC would be better served getting an apartment with a secured lobby. You're above the street scum and have some measure of peace, at least in your home. And if you just want the hustle and bustle you can commute into the city from an actual nice neighborhood when you want like a sane person and dodge most of the stupid taxes.

62033745
>>62013830
JOGGERS

>>62013874
Also this.

62033868
>>62013830
>15M to live in a dirty noisy busy smokey city filled to the brim with niggers, crime and filth
>2M to live in bucolic countryside estate and manage your investments from there
Anon...
Manhattan housing, save for tiny flats for CEOs who live in houses smaller than my living room (but "live the life"), is just tulips. If nobody is buying at 15M, you have seen the top and it's time to short sell.

item.title: Job Market KMS — 30 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Job Market KMS

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-26 08:57 UTC

OP

Nah fr what the fuck do I have to do to get a fucking job man.

i'm not here to just vent and woe is me blah blah blah but I swear to god my path leading up to this point has only put me at a disadvantage.

went to an ivy league school bc I got a massive scholarship, felt compelled to as people where I'm from don't get that opportunity all that often.

Still had to wait tables to house and feed myself. No time for internships.

Graduate 2020. Perfect timing.

Do what I need to do to make ends meet.

Laid off recently.

No company will give me the time of day unless theres an alumni there.

'underqualified' for most listings bc of unconventional path 'overqualified' for any other job to just make ends meet

95% of job listings are fake.

Fuck me.

Need legit tips. Somewhere to look I haven't thought of, someone's dick I need to suck. Should I start selling drugs?

Early replies (first 3)

62030385
>>62030360
Find a skill people will pay you for. Stop thinking about what you want; think about what your prospective employer wants.

62030397
>>62030385
genius. plenty of skills are had, the issue is finding where to apply them.

62030414
are you a jeet that fell for the cs meme?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62030415
>>62030360
This is the worst job market probably in modern history. Information is being manipulated at high levels. If you find something stable you better hang on.

62030438
>>62030360
feels good that I didn't even go to school, never looked for a job, and still retired by browsing /biz/

62030444
>>62030414
no on both counts. i studied/have worked in advertising/marketing. Lots of soft skills, sales, coordination, building systems, planning campaigns. That type of shit.

62030446
>>62030397
>plenty of skills
That’s good to hear, but no employer offering the type of job you want is looking for a jack of all trades. Everyone has basic communication skills and knows how to use excel. What single niche skill do you have that you’ve put 10,000 hours into?

62030449
>>62030415
bro fucking tell me about it. Stable to me at this point means even a fucking retail job. Can't even find that.

62030455
>>62030360
This is probably on purpose. Let the golem struggle ultra hard so that they cherish their golem position once they finally get it and dont try no funny business.

62030458
Think if everyone was dumb asf. They wouldnt want s soda theyd want a giga big fucking soda, theyd want burgers the size if basketballs. Theyd want a stripper to serve them along with some weed or beer and then theyd go to the shooting range. The next day theyd see some monster trucks and then some ufc.
Well?

62030472
>>62030446
the 'type of job i want' is literally anything that pays. Not looking for a career. Frankly, anything utilizing the skills that I have put thousands of hours into is a waste of time, i.e., diminishing returns. Those things that make me 'special' would be better utilized building something myself. But I digress.

I'm talking about the fact that it seems like I can't even get a job at fucking Starbucks lol

62030488
>>62030360
it took me a year and a half and i got lucky some mid 30s burned out had a midlife crisis so now i have a sick job. my advice is redo your resume with ai. focus it on a skill like: regulation, leadership, analytics, instrumentation..etc.

62030495
>>62030472
I’m sorry to hear you’re having such trouble finding a job anon. I hope you consider my advice if a year goes by and you still can’t find a good position.

62030539
>>62030360
Yeah anon I know, I'm sorry. I too graduated with a bachelors into 2020. Fortunately for me I was the recipient of some nepotism. It's pretty bleak out there rn. I legit think people are soon going to have trouble affording food. Idk what to do about it.

62030905
>>62030360
I moved across the country, booked Airbnb, got security guard job, used offer letter to get cheap apt, then got promoted to supervisor literally for just showing up on time everyday and being polite and friendly to my account manager.

In this supposed HCOL city I’m doing completely fine. Rent is $820, total monthly living expenses are about $1600 on the high end

If you can’t find a job it’s because you suck ass

62030957
>>62030360
See if ULINE has anything for you

62030977
>>62030360
getting a job is impossible now. I applied to 30 positions with less experience and a bachelors years ago and got a job with only two interviews

now im at 300 applied with one interview

62031000
>>62030449
same even retail jobs ghost, its kinda wild. But when i walk into any of these retail or shit jobs its filled with mexicans who dont speak english

its insane

62031647
>>62030472
you're either going to rape yourself to build skills or get raped in shitty job because you have no skills.

62033228
>>62030472
>Frankly, anything utilizing the skills that I have put thousands of hours into is a waste of time
Your skills are based on your talents, and your talents aren't going away anytime soon. They're natural to you, and the advantages you gained in whatever you spent your time doing are highly competitive against those who did not do those things. They will never catch up to you. You got a scholarship for a reason.

I applied for a job at a huge company called Syneos that also scripts and shoots pharmaceutical advertisements. Maybe find a marketing department at a company like that? I mean, I was a shoe in for this job last month. Interviewed, passed their screening exam, and was salivating for that biweekly WFH income again. I've been unemployed since Autumn '24. And I got rejected. The recruiter wanted me in 'cuz alumni and trust and cuz I was fully, unequivocally qualified on paper for this extremely low paying position. But her boss bounced it back to the head of the department they were hiring for, who, my guess is, wants to pick a superstar mindblowing applicant from a ceiling tall pile of resumes? They refreshed the job ad, so that makes me think along those lines. But I don't blame them, they're just greedy right now with a flood of juicy resumes.

Still, a lot of these people don't fucking know how to hire in the first place. I could tell by looking at the shitty work that was left behind by their previous person. Some of the hiring decisions are actually so arbitrary that it's a joke to feel ashamed to be unemployed rn. Companies waste so much money on failed initiatives and consultants. It's mostly all bullshit.

62033235
>>62030360
Imagine posting this and not posting your experience and degree. You're either a demoralization bot or so fucking retarded that you don't even know how to ask for help right and deserve homelessness and death by starvation or drug addiction.

62033297
>>62033235
not OP but it took me 18 months to find a developer job with a master's degree and 10+ years of experience (6 of those being at a famous buzzword company)
granted that was with no networking to speak of (i'm posting here after all) but yeah just getting to talk to someone without a connection is worse than a fucking crapshoot nowadays

62033363
>>62030360
>No company will give me the time of day unless there's an alumni there.
That's how I got my current job. But work at a staffing agency while you're applying for what you want. If you can't land a temp job then you probably have mental issues the state can pay for.

62033371
>>62030360
I have a security clearance, I make 25 dollars an hour scanning papers for negros.

62033383
>>62030360
Don't sell drugs. It'll just make you hate everybody and then you'll really want to rope.

62033388
You fell for the school meme, but you can’t accept your fell for it.

62033436
>>62030360
Same boat as you OP. Got laid off almost a year ago and still haven't found a job. People are always telling me to learn new skills while I'm still unemployed but how the fuck do you even do that? Enroll in a cert course? I get told that employers value experience way more so I don't even know if I'm just going to be wasting money in an attempt to "upskill" myself.

62033531
>>62033436
Most jobs value experience yes. Personally every job I have ever gotten (6 since 2022, including 4 contract positions on top of 2 full-time jobs) has been due to me lying on my resume about my experience. I was an unemployed college graduate since the end of 2020 before I got my first real job 15 months later.

62033733
>>62033531
I might have to start lying in my resume and interviews then. Fuck this world, it never pays to be honest.

62033991
>>62033235
I am not going to dox myself on fucking 4chan lol.

>>62033228
You are right. At the end of the day its just a numbers game I suppose but fuck me it's just so insane.

>>62033388
I would've really fell for it if I actually paid for it. Also checkd.

item.content:encoded:
PUMP IT

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:01 UTC

OP

WERE GOING TO 80K

Early replies (first 3)

62034846
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62034882
>loses 2 days worth of gains
>we're back!
Good luck with that.

62034937
>mumus are AIJeets
Color me shocked

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62034966
>>62034937
sybau

item.title: NO NO NO — 2 replies — 2.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-26 15:24 UTC

OP

EET'S DAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAMPING (again)

Early replies (first 3)

62035053
>>62035022
Isn't that a good thing though?

62035065
>>62035022
I voted for this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)
